tickets.title: Name the Lens: the square drawn in the glossary, taught in the tutorial (divergence preserved as a follow-on)
tickets.body:
BUILD-READY, DOCS + TUTORIAL, no src/ behavior change. Decided with Myk in chat (2026-07-17): the
word "lens" — the spec's most load-bearing piece of prose — gets NAMED, DRAWN, and DEMONSTRATED so
nobody is ever confused about it again. And the lens-divergence idea is preserved here as a taught
concept and a named follow-on, deliberately NOT built as a tool (no consumer exists yet — see the
follow-on clause).

THE CONCEPT TO NAME (settled in chat, restate it exactly): a LENS is not a type — it is the prose
name for the whole reading-side assembly. The four exported types form a square with two altitudes:

    living:   HyperSchema --x ground--> HyperView --x Schema--> View
                 | reifies                             | reifies
    frozen:   VersionedHyperSchema (FUTURE)         VersionedSchema (name@hash)

- HyperSchema: the gather program (which deltas, bucketed how). HyperView: the gathered evidence,
  all voices present. Schema: the resolution program ({props: Map<field, Policy>, default}) — which
  claims win, what fields mean. View: the resolved answer.
- The generating arrows each take a second input: HyperSchema x ground -> HyperView; Schema x
  HyperView -> View. Nothing generates from a definition alone.
- A LENS is one choice of rung per column PLUS the binding's stance (roots, writable; resolvers fold
  into the versioned rung per SS22.4). The LIVING lens evolves and is what the latest door serves; a
  PINNED lens reads through frozen rungs forever (SS17/SS23). SS26's asOf pins the ground itself —
  orthogonal to both.
- The square is currently ASYMMETRIC on purpose: VersionedSchema is built; VersionedHyperSchema is
  the named-deferred rung (spec/21 — "build it when a pin needs the gather frozen"). Draw it as
  pencil, say so.
- Forking at ANY rung yields a different lens (T2's coexistence is the rung-3 case: two Schemas over
  one gather). Lens identity is a tuple of content addresses, one per rung — the system already
  mints every one of them.

DO:
1. spec/18-glossary.md gains the LENS entry — the square drawn (ASCII, as above), the two altitudes,
   the five rungs, the it-is-prose-not-a-type rule and WHY (vocabulary must not fork into
   near-synonyms; CLAUDE.md's standing rule cites this). Cross-reference SS4, SS17, SS21, SS22, SS26.
2. spec/21-schema-identity.md gains a short "the lens, drawn" passage near the ladder (edit-in-place,
   the rare correction case): the same square, one paragraph, pointing at the glossary for the full
   treatment. Do NOT restate the ladder — link the two pictures.
3. THE TUTORIAL ACT (demos/tutorial — SS19's needs-before-doctrine voice): a lesson where the reader
   builds one gather, wears TWO lenses over it (the village's Townbook/FirstImpressions shape,
   miniaturized), pins one (name@hash), evolves the other, and SEES the square come apart and hold:
   the pinned reading never moves, the living one does, the ground is shared. Close the lesson with
   the DIVERGENCE MOMENT: print the two lenses' rung-hashes side by side (termHash, lineage,
   snapshot hash, roots) and let the reader see "these agree down to rung 3" with their own eyes —
   membership of the concept without an API. Follow demos/tutorial/lessons.mjs's existing lesson
   shape; run the tutorial to verify the lesson actually executes.
4. demos/village/README.md ledger entry if (and only if) the village is touched — the tutorial is
   the demonstration surface here; the village already demonstrates coexistence itself.

THE NAMED FOLLOW-ON (preserve verbatim, do not build): LENS DIVERGENCE as a first-class surface — a
comparison of two lenses reporting the deepest differing rung (1 gather body / 2 gather pin [waits
for VersionedHyperSchema] / 3 resolution lineage / 4 resolution pin / 5 binding stance), computed
purely from already-minted content addresses. Decided in chat (Myk, 2026-07-17): it graduates to its
own ticket WHEN a consumer arrives — the promote-LAW review (SS24.4) and federation-drift tooling are
the anticipated callers; a divergence PROFILE (which rungs differ), not a scalar metric, is the
honest shape. Until then the tutorial's printed comparison IS the feature.

DO NOT: mint a Lens type or a lensDistance API; renumber sections; restate the SS21 ladder in the
glossary (link it); let the tutorial lesson exceed the existing lessons' length register.

RAILS: the tutorial's own harness (the arc test over lessons) must stay green with the new lesson
executing; prettier/lint clean; no src/ diff at all (the ticket's boundary — a src change found
necessary is NEWS, stop and surface it).

MERGE: docs + tutorial only, no behavior, no migration -> self-mergeable once green and P5-clean.

tickets.title: LARDER — the first real Loam app: a household commons shipped as a delta-bundle in a Claude skill
tickets.body:
BUILD-READY, demos-only (no src/ change expected). Decided with Myk in chat (2026-07-18): the
first REAL Loam app — a celebration piece. Object-level and aggressively mundane on the surface
(a household grocery/pantry commons), with the platform's hidden capabilities surfacing only as
"wait, how did it do that?": no server, no signup, apps distributed as delta-bundles inside a
Claude skill, installed by conversation.

THE PITCH (keep it in the README's voice): LARDER is a shared household list. Install the skill,
tell Claude "set up our larder," and it exists — schemas, UI, and starter data all landed as
signed deltas through the loam MCP. Your partner installs, federates with one line, and now two
SOVEREIGN stores share one list. "Add milk" works from either phone via Claude; the fridge tablet
shows the renderer UI served from the store itself.

THE GROUND (larder.* contexts — domain vocabulary, never loam.*):
- Items are entities (item:<slug>); claims at (item, context): `need` (on the list), `got`
  (purchased — with a timestamp value), `have` (pantry quantity), `note`.
- The list is an entity (list:groceries) whose hyperschema gather EXPANDS item edges into child
  views (the FilmNight expandThrough pattern) so the whole list renders as one node.
- DELTA-NATIVE CHECKOFF (the philosophy, invisible): checking off your partner's item is
  OUTSAYING, not unsaying — `got` is a positive claim, and the Groceries lens resolves need-vs-got
  by latest-wins per item. Nobody negates anyone else's delta; retract-your-own stays intact; and
  "permissions" fall out of the substrate with zero authorization code.

TWO LENSES OVER ONE GROUND, DAY ONE (T2's coexistence, load-bearing and invisible):
- Groceries: the shopping list (need vs got resolved latest-wins; item children expanded).
- Pantry: what's on hand (`have`, pick-latest) — the lens future bundles read ("enough beer for
  the BBQ?" is a PARTY lens over this same ground; "italian next week" is a recipe bundle writing
  `need` claims — the composability story is ADDITIVE BUNDLES, ZERO INTEGRATION CODE; say so in
  the README).

CLAIM TEMPLATES (so Claude's writes are protocol-shaped, SPEC §14): addItem(name), gotIt(item),
stocked(item, qty), noteFor(item, text). One template call = one delta, exactly as declared.

THE RENDERER (SPEC §23): a `list` route on list:groceries — the shopping-list UI, write-enabled
(§23.3): a provisioned PEN signs the tablet's check-off form posts; the lens + route declared
public so the tablet needs no token. Keep the HTML honest and pretty-enough (this is the flashy
surface); the §23.7 byte-door is available if an icon wants to ride the store.

THE SKILL (the distribution WOW — this is the headline): demos/larder/ holds
- bundle/ — the app AS DATA: register specs (hyperschema+schema+roots+writable+mutations JSON),
  the renderer spec, seed claims. The delta-bundle a skill ships.
- SKILL.md — the Claude-facing install+drive manual: how to initialize against a running loam
  MCP (register the bundle, publish the renderer, grant the pen, declare public, seed), how to
  drive daily use (the claim templates via MCP), how a second household member joins (init +
  peers + pull — the village grow.mjs pattern), and how to answer "what do we need?" (query the
  Groceries lens). Written so a FRESH Claude with loam MCP and zero context can run the whole
  flow — that is the acceptance test of the prose.
- README.md — the celebration voice: what this is, why it could not exist elsewhere (said
  object-level: no server, no account, partner-sovereign, works offline-and-merges), and the
  future-bundles teaser (Party, Recipes) with the no-integration-code point.
- larder.mjs — the runnable proof (village-act style, check/summary harness): boots two stores
  (alice, bob), installs the bundle on both, federates, drives the daily flow (add from one
  store, check off from the other, pantry restock), asserts the list converges and the renderer
  serves on both, incl. the pen-signed form write. This is the demo's own witness; keep it green
  in the fresh-sweep ritual? NO — larder is demos/larder, not the village; give it its OWN
  one-command run documented in the README, and add it to no gate (like the village, it never
  gates the build).

DO NOT: touch src/ (a needed src change is NEWS — stop and surface it); invent a bundle FORMAT
beyond what the register/renderer doors already accept (the bundle is just their input JSONs +
seed claims — no new loader machinery in v1; Claude IS the installer, via MCP, which is the
point); build the Party or Recipe bundles (they are the sequel, named in the README).

MERGE: demos + docs only, no behavior, no migration -> self-mergeable once the runnable proof is
green and P5-clean.

DONE WHEN: demos/larder/{bundle,SKILL.md,README.md,larder.mjs} exist; larder.mjs runs green from
nothing (its own fresh homes); the SKILL.md flow has been walked end-to-end at least once by
actually following its own prose against a served store; npm run check untouched and green.
